description: Integrate LDDB into terminology and translation support
known_blockers: LDDB source authority, maintenance status, license, and access path require confirmation.
No payload, derived evidence, identifier mapping, or translation comparison should be planned until the authoritative source is identified.
expected_blockers: If source authority cannot be verified, this track may remain documentation-only.
English-only or limited-access status may reduce translation value.
blocker_owner: track implementer until reassigned in Phase 0
fallback_path: Keep LDDB as an investigation track and close as not-actionable if authority cannot be verified.

evidence_score_policy.promotion_rule: no ontology or LLM signal promotes a translation without human review

restricted_payload_policy.commit_policy: commit metadata, schemas, bounded non-restricted samples, validation summaries, review-pack structure, and derived reports only after source terms are verified; raw, credentialed, private, full-release, and licensed payloads stay local-only
required_evidence: local-only manifest if any restricted source is touched
redaction or exclusion proof before commit

fail_fast_probe.scope: one authoritative source record, one normalized registry row, one provenance row, one candidate comparison or crosswalk edge when applicable
success_criteria: source authority and license state recorded
payload handling path classified as commit-safe or local-only
schema validation passes for the bounded sample
review pack marks LLM-derived content as candidate-only when present

source_governance_result.next_allowed_action: identify authoritative LDDB source, maintenance status, access path, and redistribution terms
